title: Kataifi bird's nests
subtitle: Shredded filo nests with nut filling and honey syrup
excerpt: Small individual nests made from kataifi (shredded filo pastry) wrapped around a filling of ground almonds, pistachios, and cinnamon, then baked until golden and crunchy, and drenched in a honey and rosewater syrup. The nest shape is formed by wrapping a portion of shredded pastry around a nut ball in a ring mould or a bun tin, and comes out clean and defined once baked.
sourceNotes: Kataifi pastry (also kadayif or kunafa) is documented in Ottoman and Levantine food literature from at least the fifteenth century. Türabi Efendi, Turkish Cookery Book (1862, public domain), describes shredded pastry preparations with nut fillings and sugar syrups. The bird's nest format, in which the shredded pastry is shaped around a nut filling before baking, is described in Greek, Turkish, and Lebanese domestic pastry guides published before 1928.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bake the nests up to 2 days ahead and store in an airtight container unsoaked. Pour the syrup over just before serving; pre-soaked nests go soggy.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: conventional oven. Fan at 160°C. Bake for 22 to 26 minutes until evenly golden. The shredded pastry colours quickly; check from 18 minutes.

glossaryTerms.definition: Separating a portion of kataifi (shredded filo) into loose strands, pressing a ball of nut filling in the centre, and wrapping the strands around it to form a hollow nest shape with the filling seated inside. The key is not to pack the pastry too tightly: loose strands give a more open, crisp texture when baked, while tightly packed nests can be doughy in the centre. Brush generously with melted butter so every strand is coated; unbuttered strands will dry out rather than crisping.

content.text: Kataifi is sold in the chilled section of Middle Eastern and specialist food shops, or by mail order, as a bundle of very fine shredded pastry threads. Keep it covered with a damp cloth when not in use.
is straightforward; the critical step is buttering every strand so the pastry crisps evenly.

items.prepNote: kataifi (shredded filo), kept covered with a damp cloth
finely chopped, plus extra for decoration

content.text: Heat the oven to 180°C conventional (160°C fan). Grease a 12-hole muffin tin. Combine the ground almonds, pistachios, sugar, and cinnamon. Roll into 12 equal balls. Separate the kataifi into 12 loose portions. Drizzle each portion with melted butter and toss gently to coat the strands. Press a nut ball into the centre of each portion and wrap the strands around it, forming a nest shape. Press into the greased muffin holes. Brush the top generously with remaining melted butter.

Bake for 22 to 26 minutes until evenly golden. Meanwhile, make the syrup: combine the sugar, water, and honey in a small saucepan, bring to the boil, then simmer for 3 minutes. Remove from the heat and stir in the rosewater and lemon juice. As soon as the nests come from the oven, spoon the hot syrup over them generously, letting it soak in. Leave to cool in the tin for 20 minutes before transferring. Scatter with extra chopped pistachios.
